name: DOGE 4h Session Impulse Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: a session-window directional impulse establishes control, the first structural pullback holds, and continuation expands from that held control zone
thesis: Cycle 82 still wants an explore basket inside DOGE/ETH/TAO 4h, but the last five cycles say sparse reclaim theater is dead. Session-impulse hold expansion is a denser, more tradeable transitional mechanism for DOGE 4h because it asks for a recurring control event plus a simple hold, not a rare multi-step rescue. The edge is the held session control, not the first breakout tick.
invalidation_condition: If DOGE 4h session impulses still do not trade often enough or fail immediately after the first pullback hold, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional session impulse sets control, the first retrace holds above fast structure, and price continues into the next expansion leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition markets often hand off directional control around session boundaries. If that first pullback holds, trapped countertrend inventory can fuel a second leg with better tradeability than rare reclaim setups.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the best fit because it is the live primary lane, sits in transition, and should express session-control behavior more often than ETH chop or late TAO continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Entries may still be late if the impulse is already exhausted by the time the hold confirms.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is healthy but runner contribution is weak, move more size to the first partial before changing the impulse-hold grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of impulse-hold entries, several first-target wins, and a smaller number of larger continuation bursts.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h clears screen density and produces positive first-target expectancy with acceptable lane fit.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated impulse-hold entries that lose session control immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because the control event and hold are simple, recurring structures

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit transition lane for session impulse continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h shows enough impulse-hold expansions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h session impulses remain too sparse or fail immediately after the hold.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with several continuation bursts and a few larger runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same session-hold grammar survives on a flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves a smaller but tradable impulse-hold structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept needs more transition than pure chop offers.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, lower count continuation than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether stronger trend contamination improves or degrades the same session-control grammar.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h keeps enough impulse-hold continuation without turning into late chase entries.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h converts the setup into exhausted continuation buying.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger carry than DOGE.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.10 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.10 * ATR_14
